claim: AutoGen enables building decision agents and structured JSON agents without extensive prompt engineering by combining multi-agent orchestration with deterministic control flow
actionable: Use AutoGen's multi-agent framework with conversation types and validators to build agents that make structured decisions and return valid JSON without prompt engineering workarounds
source_title: Using AUTOGEN & GUIDANCE to code LLM Control Flow & JSON Agents (No Prompt Engineering)

claim: Guidance library allows specifying JSON schemas and control flow constraints in prompts, eliminating inconsistent LLM responses when requesting structured data
actionable: Use Guidance to define JSON schemas and if/else logic in your prompts to guarantee structured, validated responses from LLMs
source_title: Using AUTOGEN & GUIDANCE to code LLM Control Flow & JSON Agents (No Prompt Engineering)

claim: A control flow agent that validates prompts before execution prevents downstream teams from wasting money running expensive LLM calls on invalid inputs
actionable: Add a validation agent to your pipeline that checks user input validity before calling expensive models like GPT-4
source_title: Using AUTOGEN & GUIDANCE to code LLM Control Flow & JSON Agents (No Prompt Engineering)

claim: Combining structured output (from Guidance) with agentic systems prevents prompt engineering issues where 'please respond in this JSON format' produces inconsistent results
actionable: When building agentic systems, use Guidance constraints alongside AutoGen's multi-agent orchestration to ensure consistency in structured outputs
source_title: Using AUTOGEN & GUIDANCE to code LLM Control Flow & JSON Agents (No Prompt Engineering)
